message:
fix(ops): gracefully abort and join telemetry persistence worker on timeout

Add persistence_abort AtomicBool to TelemetryRuntimeHandle so the drain
timeout path can explicitly signal the persistence worker to stop before
joining it. Previously, if the drain timeout fired, the persistence worker
could be silently orphaned. Now the join() method always ensures the worker
is aborted and joined, preventing resource leaks and ensuring all buffered
telemetry is either flushed or explicitly abandoned.

- Add persistence_abort: Arc<AtomicBool> field to TelemetryRuntimeHandle
- Refactor join() to abort persistence on deadline timeout before joining
- Ensure persistence worker is always joined even if timeout fires
- Clarify documentation: drain timeout now explicitly aborts worker before join

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
